test_plan.strategy: Unit tests for all logic FRs using temp directories (os.tmpdir()) for file fixtures. Integration tests for full readAitriState + evaluateAlerts pipeline. E2E tests exercise the collection pipeline end-to-end writing to a temp dashboard.json. No hardcoded absolute paths — all fixtures created in os.tmpdir() and cleaned up in teardown.
test_plan.coverage_goal: 100% of MUST FRs with ≥3 TCs each

test_cases.title: readAitriState — returns aitriVersion '0.1.63' when present in .aitri
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri file written with JSON: { projectName:'test', aitriVersion:'0.1.63', updatedAt:'2026-03-17T23:00:00.000Z', createdAt:'2025-11-01T14:00:00.000Z', currentPhase:2, approvedPhases:[1], completedPhases:[1] }
test_cases.given: a .aitri file at /tmp/tc-010h/.aitri containing { aitriVersion: '0.1.63', updatedAt: '2026-03-17T23:00:00.000Z', createdAt: '2025-11-01T14:00:00.000Z' }
test_cases.then: returned object has aitriVersion === '0.1.63', updatedAt === '2026-03-17T23:00:00.000Z', createdAt === '2025-11-01T14:00:00.000Z'
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix
Write .aitri JSON with aitriVersion, updatedAt, createdAt fields

test_cases.title: readAitriState — returns aitriVersion null when field absent (pre-v0.1.20 project)
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri file written with JSON: { projectName:'legacy-project', currentPhase:3, approvedPhases:[1,2], completedPhases:[1,2] } — no aitriVersion, no updatedAt, no createdAt fields
test_cases.given: a .aitri file at /tmp/tc-010f/.aitri containing { projectName: 'legacy-project', currentPhase: 3, approvedPhases: [1,2] } with no aitriVersion, updatedAt, or createdAt fields
test_cases.then: returned object has aitriVersion === null, updatedAt === null, createdAt === null — and no exception is thrown
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix
Write .aitri JSON without aitriVersion, updatedAt, createdAt
test_cases.expected_result: result.aitriVersion === null, result.updatedAt === null, result.createdAt === null; no exception thrown

test_cases.title: readAitriState — returns aitriVersion null when field is a number (type coercion guard)
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri file written with JSON: { aitriVersion: 163, currentPhase: 1 } — aitriVersion is a number, not a string
test_cases.given: a .aitri file at /tmp/tc-010e/.aitri containing { aitriVersion: 163 } where aitriVersion is the number 163 (not the string '0.1.63')
test_cases.then: returned object has aitriVersion === null — only string type is accepted per integration contract
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix
Write .aitri JSON with aitriVersion as a number: 163

test_cases.title: readAitriState — preserves artifactsDir 'spec' when explicitly set
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri file written with JSON: { projectName: 'hub', artifactsDir: 'spec', currentPhase: 1, approvedPhases: [], completedPhases: [] }
test_cases.given: a .aitri file at /tmp/tc-011h/.aitri containing { artifactsDir: 'spec', projectName: 'hub' }
test_cases.then: returned object has artifactsDir === 'spec' and projectName === 'hub'
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix

test_cases.title: readAitriState — defaults artifactsDir to '' (not 'spec') when field absent
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri file written with JSON: { projectName: 'adopted-project', currentPhase: 2, approvedPhases: [1], completedPhases: [1] } — no artifactsDir field
test_cases.given: a .aitri file at /tmp/tc-011f/.aitri with no artifactsDir field (adopted project, pre-v0.1.20 format)
test_cases.then: returned object has artifactsDir === '' (empty string) — NOT 'spec'
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix
test_cases.expected_result: result.artifactsDir === '' (strictly empty string, not 'spec' or undefined)

test_cases.title: readAitriState — defaults projectName to basename of projectDir when field absent
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir() with suffix '/finance-tracker'; .aitri file written with JSON: { currentPhase: 1, approvedPhases: [], completedPhases: [] } — no projectName field
test_cases.given: a .aitri file at os.tmpdir()+'/finance-tracker/.aitri' with no projectName field, and projectDir is the path ending in 'finance-tracker'
test_cases.then: returned object has projectName === 'finance-tracker' (path.basename of projectDir)
steps: Create temp dir path ending in 'finance-tracker' using path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'finance-tracker')

test_cases.title: detectDrift — returns true immediately from driftPhases fast path (no disk read)
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri written with driftPhases: ['2'], approvedPhases: [1, 2], artifactHashes: {}
No artifact files created in the temp dir (verifies no disk read occurs)
test_cases.given: a .aitri file containing { driftPhases: ['2'], approvedPhases: [1, 2], artifactHashes: {} } and NO artifact files on disk
test_cases.then: returned object has hasDrift === true — fast path triggered without reading any artifact file
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix
Write .aitri JSON with driftPhases: ['2'], approvedPhases: [1, 2], artifactHashes: {}
Do NOT create any artifact files in the temp dir
test_cases.expected_result: result.hasDrift === true (no artifact files needed — driftPhases fast path returned early)

test_cases.title: detectDrift — driftPhases empty array does not trigger drift
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri written with driftPhases: [], approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: {} (no stored hash for phase 1)
test_cases.given: a .aitri file containing { driftPhases: [], approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: {} } — driftPhases is an empty array
test_cases.then: returned object has hasDrift === false — empty driftPhases does not trigger fast path; hash check finds no stored hash for phase 1 so also returns false
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix
Write .aitri JSON with driftPhases: [], approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: {}

test_cases.title: detectDrift — absent driftPhases field (pre-v0.1.58 project) falls through without throwing
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir(); .aitri written with approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: {} — no driftPhases field
test_cases.given: a .aitri file containing { approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: {} } with NO driftPhases field (project created before v0.1.58)
test_cases.then: call completes without throwing TypeError or ReferenceError; result is a valid AitriState object; hasDrift === false (no stored hash, no files to compare)
steps: Create temp dir using os.tmpdir() + unique suffix
Write .aitri JSON with approvedPhases: [1], artifactHashes: {}, no driftPhases key

test_cases.title: detectDrift — returns false when artifact on disk matches stored sha256 hash
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir()
temp/spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json written with content '{"project_name":"test"}'
sha256 of that exact content computed via crypto.createHash('sha256').update('{"project_name":"test"}').digest('hex') and stored as artifactHashes['1']
.aitri written with approvedPhases: [1], artifactsDir: 'spec', artifactHashes: { '1': '<computed-sha256>' }
test_cases.given: a project at tempDir with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing '{"project_name":"test"}' and .aitri.artifactHashes['1'] == sha256('{"project_name":"test"}')
test_cases.then: result.hasDrift === false — artifact content matches stored hash
steps: Create tempDir and tempDir/spec/ using os.tmpdir()
Compute sha256 of that content in test setup
Write tempDir/.aitri with approvedPhases:[1], artifactsDir:'spec', artifactHashes:{'1': computedHash}

test_cases.title: detectDrift — returns true when artifact on disk differs from stored sha256 hash
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir()
temp/spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json written with content '{"project_name":"modified"}'

.aitri written with approvedPhases:[1], artifactsDir:'spec', artifactHashes:{ '1': 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa' } (stale hash, 64 hex chars, does not match current file)
test_cases.given: a project at tempDir with spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json containing '{"project_name":"modified"}' and .aitri.artifactHashes['1'] === 'aaaa...aaaa' (64-char hex string that does NOT match the current file's sha256)
test_cases.then: result.hasDrift === true — current sha256 of the file does not match stored hash
steps: Create tempDir and tempDir/spec/ using os.tmpdir()
Write tempDir/.aitri with approvedPhases:[1], artifactsDir:'spec', artifactHashes:{'1': 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa'}

test_cases.title: detectDrift — approved phase with no stored hash is NOT counted as drifted
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir()
.aitri written with approvedPhases:[1], artifactHashes:{} (empty — phase 1 approved before v0.1.51 when hashes were introduced)
No artifact file created
test_cases.given: a .aitri file with approvedPhases:[1] and artifactHashes:{} — phase 1 is approved but has no stored hash (old project pre-v0.1.51)
test_cases.then: result.hasDrift === false — a missing hash means the phase was never hashed, not that it drifted
steps: Do NOT create any artifact files

test_cases.title: detectDrift — artifact file missing on disk returns false without throwing
preconditions: temp dir created via os.tmpdir()
.aitri written with approvedPhases:[1], artifactsDir:'spec', artifactHashes:{'1':'bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb'}
spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json does NOT exist
test_cases.given: a .aitri with artifactHashes:{'1':'bbbb...bbbb'} (64-char hex) and approvedPhases:[1], but spec/01_REQUIREMENTS.json does not exist on disk
test_cases.then: result.hasDrift === false and no exception is thrown — missing file treated as unverifiable, not drifted
steps: Do NOT create tempDir/spec/ or any artifact files

test_cases.title: evaluateAlerts — generates VERSION_MISMATCH warning when project version differs from installed CLI
preconditions: Module-level version cache in engine.js must be reset to undefined before this test
aitri --version subprocess must be stubbed/mocked to return '0.1.63' (or test runs in env where aitri CLI is installed at 0.1.63)
test_cases.when: evaluateAlerts({ aitriState: { aitriVersion: '0.1.50', verifyPassed: true, hasDrift: false, verifySummary: null }, gitMeta: { isGitRepo: true, lastCommitAgeHours: 1 }, testSummary: { available: false }, cacheStale: false, complianceSummary: { available: false } }) is called
test_cases.then: returned array contains exactly one alert with type === 'version-mismatch', severity === 'warning', message === 'Aitri version mismatch: project 0.1.50, CLI 0.1.63'
steps: Reset engine module version cache to undefined
Call evaluateAlerts with data where aitriState.aitriVersion === '0.1.50'
Assert alerts array contains an entry with type: 'version-mismatch'
Assert that entry has message: 'Aitri version mismatch: project 0.1.50, CLI 0.1.63'
test_cases.expected_result: alerts includes { type: 'version-mismatch', severity: 'warning', message: 'Aitri version mismatch: project 0.1.50, CLI 0.1.63' }

test_cases.title: evaluateAlerts — no VERSION_MISMATCH alert when aitriVersion is null
preconditions: Module-level version cache reset to undefined
getInstalledAitriVersion stubbed to return '0.1.63'
test_cases.given: data.aitriState.aitriVersion === null (pre-v0.1.20 project with no aitriVersion in .aitri) and installed CLI is '0.1.63'
test_cases.when: evaluateAlerts({ aitriState: { aitriVersion: null, verifyPassed: true, hasDrift: false, verifySummary: null }, gitMeta: { isGitRepo: true, lastCommitAgeHours: 1 }, testSummary: { available: false }, cacheStale: false, complianceSummary: { available: false } }) is called
test_cases.then: returned array contains zero alerts with type === 'version-mismatch'
steps: Call evaluateAlerts with data where aitriState.aitriVersion === null
Assert no alert in returned array has type === 'version-mismatch'

test_cases.title: evaluateAlerts — no VERSION_MISMATCH alert and no crash when aitri CLI is not installed (ENOENT)
preconditions: Module-level version cache reset to undefined
execFileSync for 'aitri --version' stubbed to throw { code: 'ENOENT' }
test_cases.given: data.aitriState.aitriVersion === '0.1.50' and execFileSync('aitri', ['--version']) throws Error with code 'ENOENT' (CLI not installed)
test_cases.when: evaluateAlerts({ aitriState: { aitriVersion: '0.1.50', verifyPassed: true, hasDrift: false, verifySummary: null }, gitMeta: { isGitRepo: true, lastCommitAgeHours: 1 }, testSummary: { available: false }, cacheStale: false, complianceSummary: { available: false } }) is called
test_cases.then: call completes without throwing; returned array contains zero alerts with type === 'version-mismatch'
steps: Stub execFileSync to throw { code: 'ENOENT', message: 'spawn aitri ENOENT' }
Assert no exception thrown by evaluateAlerts
Assert no alert with type 'version-mismatch' in result

test_cases.title: evaluateAlerts — aitri --version called at most once for 20 consecutive evaluateAlerts calls (cache hit)
preconditions: Module-level version cache reset to undefined
execFileSync stubbed with a call counter; returns '0.1.63' on first call
test_cases.given: 20 project data objects each with aitriState.aitriVersion === '0.1.50' and execFileSync stubbed with a call counter initialized to 0
test_cases.when: evaluateAlerts is called 20 times sequentially (once per project data object)
test_cases.then: execFileSync('aitri', ['--version']) was invoked exactly 1 time total — subsequent calls used the cached result
steps: Create spy/stub that increments callCount on each execFileSync invocation and returns '0.1.63'
test_cases.expected_result: execFileSync called exactly once; callCount === 1 after 20 evaluateAlerts invocations

test_cases.title: TC-010e2e: Full collection cycle — aitriVersion flows from .aitri to dashboard.json entry
preconditions: temp project dir created via os.tmpdir() with .aitri containing { projectName: 'e2e-proj', aitriVersion: '0.1.50', currentPhase: 2, approvedPhases: [1], completedPhases: [1] }
temp hub dir created via os.tmpdir() with projects.json registering the temp project as a local entry
Collection cycle executed against temp hub dir
test_cases.given: a registered project at tempProjectDir with .aitri.aitriVersion === '0.1.50' and projects.json at tempHubDir pointing to it
test_cases.when: the collector runs one full cycle (collectAll + writeDashboard) writing to tempHubDir/dashboard.json
test_cases.then: tempHubDir/dashboard.json is valid JSON; the entry for 'e2e-proj' contains aitriVersion === '0.1.50'
steps: Create tempProjectDir via os.tmpdir(); write .aitri with aitriVersion: '0.1.50'
Create tempHubDir via os.tmpdir(); write projects.json with one local entry pointing to tempProjectDir
Run the collector: import collectAll + writeDashboard, pass tempHubDir as the store path
Read tempHubDir/dashboard.json and parse as JSON

test_cases.title: TC-014e3e: Full collection cycle — VERSION_MISMATCH alert present in dashboard.json when versions differ
preconditions: temp project dir created via os.tmpdir() with .aitri containing { projectName: 'version-test', aitriVersion: '0.1.01', currentPhase: 1, approvedPhases: [], completedPhases: [] }
temp hub dir created via os.tmpdir() with projects.json registering the temp project
aitri CLI installed and version > '0.1.01' (test environment has aitri ≥ 0.1.50 installed)
test_cases.given: a registered project with aitriVersion: '0.1.01' in .aitri and aitri CLI installed at a version > '0.1.01'
test_cases.when: the collector runs one full cycle writing to tempHubDir/dashboard.json
test_cases.then: dashboard.json entry for 'version-test' has alerts array containing an entry with type === 'version-mismatch' and severity === 'warning'
steps: Create tempProjectDir via os.tmpdir(); write .aitri with aitriVersion: '0.1.01'
Create tempHubDir via os.tmpdir(); write projects.json pointing to tempProjectDir
Assert entry.alerts contains an object with type: 'version-mismatch' and severity: 'warning'
test_cases.expected_result: dashboard.json entry alerts array contains { type: 'version-mismatch', severity: 'warning' }
